title: Why bother sending Rei to school in the first place
display_title: Why bother sending Rei to school in the first place
wikitext:
[]: Why bother sending Rei to school in the first place?             Quote Post by Rei IV » Wed Apr 24, 2013 8:01 am If Gendo's plan was to ultimately have that much control over Rei, why bother sending her to school in the first place? Why not limit her interactions with other characters? Why not monitor her behavior around the pilots, specifically Shinji, as well? He could've come up with some BS that she attends a private institution, hence (in this hypothetical scenario) why we don't see much of her and simply keep her locked up or isolated in some room at NERV headquarters. Her attachment/interest to the Shinji should've been the first sign to ring the alarms. Granted, it begs the question would he have had the same impact on her (and vice versa) if they did not attend the same schools and were in the same class, and how much would Shinji have cared for her if that were the case? You think this would've drastically changed the two pilots and make Rei more appeasing to Gendo during the initiation of the 3rd Impact in EoE?

[]:             Quote Post by Felipe » Wed Apr 24, 2013 8:36 am The way I see it is: because he cares about her, and probably wants her to have as normal and happy a life as possible.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Apr 24, 2013 9:03 am Because there's a cover story to maintain and he doesn't want to give Seele any more hints about his actual plan than he can possibly help. The less they know about Rei the better.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Wed Apr 24, 2013 9:14 am Felipe wrote: The way I see it is: because he cares about her, and probably wants her to have as normal and happy a life as possible.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Apr 24, 2013 10:12 am People would get suspicious about the uniform if she didn't actually go to school.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Apr 24, 2013 10:28 am Keeping appearances - Rei's unexplained appearance is already suspicious enough as it is. No need to breed even more suspicion . TheFriskyIan wrote: I'm not sure about the latter, I'm sure he knew about her living conditions in the apartment. However he did seem to actively care about her education. If I remember correctly, there was one episode where he asked her how she was doing in school.

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Wed Apr 24, 2013 1:19 pm Kendrix wrote: "formless invisible cues between people" (to quote EoE)

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Apr 25, 2013 6:58 pm Where else is Rei going to learn to read, write, do arithmetic, and behave in society? Seriously. Sending Rei to school is the most efficient way to actually raise her, or at least teach her how to act normally, is simply to send her to school. Along these lines, recall that Rei I probably never went to a school. Rei II only started in fifth grade, which is ~ 11 years old I believe.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sat Apr 27, 2013 8:55 pm Kendrix wrote: "formless invisible cues between people" (to quote EoE)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Apr 27, 2013 9:31 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: What I find most fitting is that the quote used is spoken by Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Sun Apr 28, 2013 11:39 am why bother sending her to school in the first place?

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Sun Apr 28, 2013 12:08 pm Why not?It wouldn't make much sense if she was in NERV all day and it would be suspicious.Gendo probably want's her to act like a normal child and become more sociable.The same goes for living in an apartment by herself.

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Wed May 01, 2013 4:55 pm Or: Truancy laws. I don't know if Japan has them, but in most US states, kids under 16 are required by law to be in some kind of school or homeschooling program. Occam's razor declares me the winner of this thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed May 01, 2013 5:02 pm Elect G-Max wrote: Occam's razor declares me the winner of this thread.

[]: Wait a minute...             Quote Post by SonGoku86 » Mon May 27, 2013 4:27 pm I just realized something... If Rei were to die, wouldn't the next one not be able to remember anything? Like when Rei II died, Rei III didn't remember saving Shinji. Wouldn't she forget everything she learned in school?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon May 27, 2013 4:31 pm Rei III did remember crying though. It seems that Nerv has some kind of "digital" backup of Rei somewhere -- in the Dummy Rei's probably -- but that still elements of her self are carried over by the recovery process anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by SonGoku86 » Mon May 27, 2013 4:38 pm Ah, I forgot about that.

[]:             Quote Post by Eye-ce » Tue May 28, 2013 1:29 am In order to bond with Shinji and complete human instrumentality, she needed to interact with him in a social space like school outside of Eva piloting. I like to believe that under Gendo's harsh exterior, he really wanted his son to be in a healthy relationship with his "mom" and thus pilot the Eva more effectively to defend Nerv.

[]:             Quote Post by xdiesp » Tue May 28, 2013 7:43 am I never really understood why the Reis in the aquarium aren't actually outside piloting everything, doing chores and running NERV. Occam's razor declares me the winner of this thread

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Tue May 28, 2013 7:58 am xdiesp wrote: Philosophy can be surprisingly stupid at times: honestly tell me you wouldn't think someone a simpleton, if he sprung up telling you that things are most often plain and simple. They aren't.
